$comment: WITS is a Qt 6 / C++17 / CMake project. A user-level 'kane-claude-standalone'
install ships same-named, Next.js/React/TypeScript-oriented skills that have
shadowed this repo's Qt-adapted copies in practice (a /create-pr run once
dispatched a four-agent gate including api-checker, which this repo does not
define). See the 'Skill precedence' section of CLAUDE.md.
The deny list below names ONLY skills that this project does not define and
that superpowers provides a Qt-safe equivalent for. Denying the bare name
forces the explicit 'superpowers:'-prefixed skill, which is unambiguous.
commit, claude-review, codex-review, create-pr - this repo DEFINES these in
.claude/skills/; denying the bare name would kill the project copy too.
Precedence for these is enforced by the CLAUDE.md tell (the project
create-pr dispatches exactly three agents, never api-checker).
merge-pr - exists only at user level, has no project or superpowers
equivalent, and is generic git plumbing, so it is safe as-is.
